chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD Cr. Bail Application No. S-783 of 2024 Qamber Ali...............................................................Applicant Versus The State..............................................................Respondent Mumtaz Alam Leghari, advocate for applicant Sana Memon, Assistant P.G Date of Hearing: 11.11.2024 ORDER YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, J - Following the dismissal of his earlier bail application by the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Shahdadpur the Applicant has approached this Court under section 497 Cr.P.C seeking post-arrest bail in respect of FIR No. 57 of 2024, registered against him at Police Station DKR @ Maqsoodo Rind on 27.08.2024 under Sections 24 of Sindh Arms Act 2013. 2. Succinctly stated allegation as per FIR against the applicant is that the interrogation of the Applicant while arrested in another FIR bearing Crime No.56 of 2024 registered under Sections 38, 381-A, 457 and 34 PPC yielded recovery of an unnumbered and unlicensed T.T pistol with two live rounds. 3. Learned counsel for the Applicant and the learned APG were heard and the record perused, with the following points coming to the fore: (a) That the applicant had already been granted bail in the main case (viz. Crime No.56 of 2024) by the Trial Court vide Order dated 07.11.2024 made on Criminal Bail Application No.1290 of 2024 with that matter being considered as one requiring the further inquiry. 2 (b) That the Applicant has no criminal record other than the aforementioned crime number in respect of which he had already been granted bail. (c) The investigation has been completed with the Applicant not being required further for that purpose or there being any prospect of tampering/interference by him. (d) No flight risk concerns were raised. 4.

Learned counsel for the Applicant and the learned APG were heard and the record perused, with the following points coming to the fore: (a) That the applicant had already been granted bail in the main case (viz. Crime No.56 of 2024) by the Trial Court vide Order dated 07.11.2024 made on Criminal Bail Application No.1290 of 2024 with that matter being considered as one requiring the further inquiry. 2 (b) That the Applicant has no criminal record other than the aforementioned crime number in respect of which he had already been granted bail. (c) The investigation has been completed with the Applicant not being required further for that purpose or there being any prospect of tampering/interference by him. (d) No flight risk concerns were raised. 4. It is in view of those factors that the Application was allowed vide a short Order made in Court upon culmination of the hearing on 11.11.2024 with the applicant being granted post-arrest bail subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.50,000/- and execution of P.R Bond in the lime amount to the satisfaction of Trial Court. JUDGE
